reasoning: Based on checking the Wikipedia page for 'FIDE rankings' as of the forecast date (March 1, 2026, though fetched via the Feb 28 revision), Anish Giri's Elo rating is listed as 2753. The FIDE rating system updates official ratings once per month, and the Wikipedia page mirrors these monthly lists. Therefore, his rating on the Wikipedia page will almost certainly remain 2753 throughout the entire month of March. A 1% increase over his March 1 rating of 2753 would require his listed rating to jump to approximately 2781 (+28 points). Since the page is not updated with live mid-month ratings, the value on March 8 will be identical to March 1 (a 0% change). By March 31, even if the April list were somehow published & updated on Wikipedia a day early, Anish Giri isn't playing a major classical tournament that finishes in March prior to the list compilation (he begins the Candidates Tournament on March 28, meaning those games will be rated later). Even if games were rated, gaining 28 Elo points in one month at the Super-GM level requires an extraordinarily dominant performance that borders on impossible for a single event. Thus, the chance of his Wiki FIDE rating being at least 1% higher is functionally zero, aside from the possibility of Wikipedia vandalism at the exact time of resolution.
